messages.content: 广泛应用的同义词包括：普遍应用、广泛使用、被广泛采用、普及使用、广泛应用、常见应用、常用的、广泛实施、被广泛应用、广泛推广等。
详细阐述火法冶金在锂电回收工艺上的应用和优缺点

火法冶金是指利用高温将原料焙烧、还原或氧化等处理方法，使得其中一种或多种元素转化成为可溶性的化合物或氧化物，从而实现其分离和提取的过程。在锂电回收工艺中，火法冶金主要应用于回收废旧锂离子电池中的有价金属材料，如钴、镍、锂等。

火法冶金在锂电回收工艺上的优点主要包括：
1. 可以有效回收废旧锂离子电池中的多种有价金属，如钴、镍、锂等，回收率较高。
2. 火法冶金可以对废旧锂离子电池进行高温焙烧，从而使电池中的有害物质得到分解和消除，降低环境污染。
3. 火法冶金工艺成熟，操作简便，生产成本相对较低。

火法冶金在锂电回收工艺上的缺点包括：
1. 火法冶金需要高温条件，能源消耗较大，会对环境造成一定的污染。
2. 火法冶金相对失误率较高，易出现过程控制不精确而导致金属回收率低的情况。
3. 火法冶金生产过程中产生的烟尘、废气和废水等都是环境污染物，需要进行处理和排放。

综上所述，火法冶金作为锂电回收的一种常用工艺，有其独特的优势和不足之处，在实际应用中需要根据具体情况综合考虑利弊，选择合适的回收工艺和实施措施。
以综述的形式概括该文，Bioleaching of metals from spent lithium ion secondary  batteries using Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans.

该文综述了利用嗜酸性铁氧化杆菌(Acidithiobacillus ferrooxidans)进行废旧锂离子二次电池的生物浸出提取金属的研究。文章介绍了生物浸出过程的机理和影响该过程的因素。作者指出，在适当的反应条件下(如 pH、温度和浸出时间)，可以有效地提高金属浸出率，这种生物浸出过程对于提取废旧锂离子二次电池中的钴、镍等有价金属具有广泛的应用前景。然而，该方法当前还存在一些问题，比如生物反应条件的控制和菌群的稳定性等，这些问题需要进一步的研究和解决。总的来说，该文为利用生物技术回收金属材料的研究提供了有益的指导和启示。
以综述的形式概括该文，Selective recovery of valuable metals from spent Li-ion  batteries using solvent-impregnated resins

本文综述了采用溶剂浸渍树脂进行废旧锂离子电池有价金属的选择性回收的研究。作者介绍了溶剂浸渍树脂的工作原理及其物理化学特性，并讨论了该技术在回收钴、镍等金属方面的应用。文章指出，溶剂浸渍树脂在回收锂离子电池中的有价金属时，具有选择性好、反应速度快、操作方便等优点。此外，文章还介绍了溶剂浸渍树脂在实际应用过程中的一些问题和挑战，如选择合适的树脂材料和溶剂、与电池残留物反应产生的影响等。为了解决这些问题，作者提出了提高回收率、改进树脂材料合成、改进溶剂配方以及优化操作条件等方面的建议。总的来说，文章为废旧锂离子电池中有价金属回收技术的研究提供了有益的思路和参考。
以综述形式概括该文，Electrochemical and structural characterization of cobalt recycled from cathodes of spent Li-ion batteries

本文综述了利用电化学技术回收废旧锂离子电池阴极材料中的钴的研究。文章介绍了钴的回收过程，提出了回收过程中需要考虑的关键问题。作者还阐述了电化学技术的工作原理及其适用性，并描述了电化学池的设计和实施过程。进一步地，文章还讨论了方法的成本和效益，并对已有研究进行了综合分析和总结。结果表明，电化学回收法具有高回收率和结构稳定性，并且可以合理地利用有价钴资源。但是，该方法也存在一些技术和经济问题，如最佳电解参数的确定、电极材料的选择和电解液的成本等，这些问题需要进一步的研究。总的来说，本文为废旧锂离子电池回收钴的研究提供了有益的思路、方法和参考。

概括下列文字，并用学术性语言改善表达，n this work, cobalt from spent Li-ion batteries of cellular phones was recycled using electrochemical techniques. The efficiency, structure and morphology of deposits were influenced by the pH solution and charge density. Maximum efficiency is obtained for pH = 5.40 for all charge densities analyzed. The presence of the cobalt hcp phase for both pH solutions (5.40 and 2.70) and charge density conditions (10.0 and 50.0 C cm−2) used in the electrodeposition process was detected by X-ray diffraction. The growth of the cobalt deposit is favored in the crystallographic direction [002] for pH = 5.40 and in the direction [110] for pH = 2.70 when the charge density is 10.0 C cm−2. The [100] and [101] crystallographic directions of hcp phase were detected by X-ray diffractogram for both pH conditions when the charge density was increased to 50.0 C cm−2. The potentiodynamic dissolution of the cobalt depends on its structural composition.

This study aimed to recycle cobalt from spent Li-ion batteries of cellular phones through electrochemical techniques. The effects of pH solutions and charge density on the efficiency, structure, and morphology of cobalt deposits were investigated. Results showed that the maximum efficiency was achieved at a pH of 5.40 for all analyzed charge densities. X-ray diffraction analysis confirmed the presence of the cobalt hcp phase in both pH solutions (5.40 and 2.70) and charge density conditions (10.0 and 50.0 C cm−2) used in the electrodeposition process. The growth of the cobalt deposit was found to be dependent on the crystallographic direction. Specifically, the [002] direction favored the growth of the cobalt deposit in pH = 5.40, while the [110] direction favored this in pH = 2.70 when the charge density was 10.0 C cm−2. When the charge density was increased to 50.0 C cm−2, the [100] and [101] crystallographic directions of hcp phase were detected by X-ray diffraction in both pH conditions. Furthermore, the potentiodynamic dissolution of the cobalt was found to be dependent on its structural composition.

概括下列文字，并用学术性语言改善表达，A closed-loop process to recover lithium carbonate from cathode scrap of lithium-ion battery (LIB) is developed. Lithium could be selectively leached into solution using formic acid while aluminum remained as the metallic form, and most of the other metals from the cathode scrap could be precipitated out. This phenomenon clearly demonstrates that formic acid can be used for lithium recovery from cathode scrap, as both leaching and separation reagent. By investigating the effects of different parameters including temperature, formic acid concentration, H2O2 amount, and solid to liquid ratio, the leaching rate of Li can reach 99.93% with minor Al loss into the solution. Subsequently, the leaching kinetics was evaluated and the controlling step as well as the apparent activation energy could be determined. After further separation of the remaining Ni, Co, and Mn from the leachate, Li2CO3 with the purity of 99.90% could be obtained. The final solution after lithium carbonate extraction can be further processed for sodium formate preparation, and Ni, Co, and Mn precipitates are ready for precursor preparation for cathode materials. As a result, the global recovery rates of Al, Li, Ni, Co, and Mn in this process were found to be 95.46%, 98.22%, 99.96%, 99.96%, and 99.95% respectively, achieving effective resources recycling from cathode scrap of spent LIB.

This work developed a closed-loop process for the recovery of lithium carbonate from cathode scrap of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Lithium was selectively leached into solution using formic acid while aluminum remained as the metallic form, and most of the other metals from the cathode scrap could be precipitated out. The study investigated the effects of different parameters, such as temperature, formic acid concentration, H2O2 amount, and solid to liquid ratio, on the leaching rate. The leaching rate of Li reached 99.93% with minor Al loss into the solution. The leaching kinetics was evaluated, and the controlling step and the apparent activation energy were determined. After further separation of the remaining Ni, Co, and Mn from the leachate, Li2CO3 with a purity of 99.90% could be obtained. The final solution after lithium carbonate extraction was processed for sodium formate preparation, and Ni, Co, and Mn precipitates were ready for precursor preparation for cathode materials. The global recovery rates of Al, Li, Ni, Co, and Mn in this process were found to be 95.46%, 98.22%, 99.96%, 99.96%, and 99.95%, respectively—resulting in effective resource recycling from cathode scrap of spent LIBs. These results clearly demonstrate that formic acid can be used as a leaching and separation reagent for lithium recovery from cathode scrap.

概括下列文字，并用学术性语言改善表达，Studies are conducted on the leaching and solvent extraction separation of metals from chloride leach liquor of spent nickel–cadmium batteries with Cyanex 923 and 272 diluted in kerosene as the extractants. Dissolution of the metals increases with increase in acid concentration and time but decreases with the solids-to-liquid ratio. Complete dissolution of Cd, Co and Ni can be achieved with 1.5 M HCl at 85 °C for 8 h and a solids-to-liquid ratio of 4. Treatment of leach liquor for the separation of metals with Cyanex 923 shows that increase of extractant and chloride ion concentration increases the percentage extraction of cadmium. The plot of log[distribution coefficient] versus log[extractant]/[Cl−] is linear with a slope of 2, which indicates that the extraction follows a solvation mechanism with the extracted species as CdCl2·2S (S, Cyanex 923). Moreover, Cyanex 923 enables a clear separation of Cd from Co and Ni. Extraction of cobalt with Cyanex 272 involves a cation-exchange mechanism with the formation of a 1:2 metal-to-ligand complex in the organic phase. Based on the distribution data, extractant concentration and equilibrium pH of the aqueous phase, a possible separation process is proposed for the recovery of cadmium, cobalt and nickel with >99% efficiency.

This study investigates the leaching and solvent extraction separation of metals from chloride leach liquor of spent nickel-cadmium batteries using Cyanex 923 and 272 diluted in kerosene as the extractants. Results suggest that dissolution of the metals increases with increases in acid concentration and time, but decreases with solids-to-liquid ratio. Full dissolution of Cd, Co, and Ni can be achieved with 1.5 M HCl at 85 °C for 8 h and a solids-to-liquid ratio of 4. Treatment of leach liquor for the separation of metals with Cyanex 923 shows that increased extractant and chloride ion concentration increase the percentage extraction of cadmium. A plot of log[distribution coefficient] versus log[extractant]/[Cl−] is linear with a slope of 2, indicating solvent extraction via a solvation mechanism with the extracted species as CdCl2·2S (S, Cyanex 923). Cyanex 923 enables a clear separation of Cd from Co and Ni. Extraction of cobalt with Cyanex 272 involves a cation-exchange mechanism, where a 1:2 metal-to-ligand complex is formed in the organic phase. Based on distribution data, extractant concentration, and equilibrium pH of the aqueous phase, a possible separation process is proposed for the recovery of cadmium, cobalt, and nickel with >99% efficiency. These findings suggest that Cyanex 923 and 272 could be effective extractants for the selective separation and recovery of metals from the chloride leach liquor of spent nickel-cadmium batteries.
以综述格式概括下面文章，并用学术性语言规范表达。Recovery of cobalt from spent lithium ion batteries using sulphuric  acid leaching followed by solid-liquid separation and solvent extraction

本文综述了采用硫酸浸出、固液分离和溶剂萃取技术从废旧锂离子电池中回收钴的研究。文章介绍了目前通常采用的废旧锂离子电池回收方法，并讨论了硫酸浸出、固液分离和溶剂萃取结合的方法在钴回收中的应用优势。作者阐述了该技术的具体实施步骤、其反应机理以及影响其回收率和选择性的关键参数。研究表明，该方法可以在较短的时间内实现高效的钴回收，并且可以解决回收过程中出现的其他金属离子对钴萃取的干扰问题。此外，作者还评估了该方法的经济效益和环境影响，并提出了未来的研究方向。综合来看，硫酸浸出、固液分离和溶剂萃取结合的方法在废旧锂离子电池中回收钴的研究中具有很大的潜力和广泛的应用前景。
